title: The this Keyword
summary: this refers to the object a function is working with.
Its value depends on how the function is called.
Getting this wrong is a common source of bugs.

sections.text: The word this is one of the most confusing parts of JavaScript, because its meaning changes depending on the situation. Once you learn the simple question to ask, it stops being scary.

sections.title: What this Means
sections.text: Inside a function, this refers to the object that the function is acting on. In a method on an object, this is usually that object. The key idea is that this is not fixed when you write the function. It is decided by how the function is called.

sections.title: Why It Trips People Up
sections.text: Because this depends on the call, the same function can have a different this in different situations. Pull a method off its object and call it alone, and this may no longer point where you expect. This is the source of many puzzling bugs, which is why it pays to slow down and ask what this refers to here.

media.alt: The same function having a different this depending on how it is called

sections.title: The Word Here
sections.text: this is like the word here in a sentence. Here means something different depending on where you are standing. Say here in the kitchen and here in the garden, and the word points to different places, even though the word never changed. this works the same way, it depends on the situation.

media.alt: The word here pointing to different places as a person moves around

sections.title: Figuring Out this
items: Find where the function is being called, not written.
If it is called as a method on an object, this is that object.
If it is called alone, this may be something else.
Ask plainly, what object is this acting on right now.
If you want this to stay fixed, an arrow function can help.

sections.text: Arrow functions do not get their own this. They borrow it from the surrounding code, which is exactly why they became popular for avoiding this confusion in callbacks.

terms.definition: The object a function is acting on.
A function attached to an object.
How a function is run, which decides this.
What this usually points to in a method.
A function that borrows this from around it.

options: The object a function is working with
questions.explanation: this refers to the object that the function is acting on.

questions.text: What decides the value of this?
questions.explanation: this is decided by how the function is called, not where it is written.

questions.text: How do arrow functions treat this?
options: They borrow it from the surrounding code
They always set it to null
questions.explanation: Arrow functions do not get their own this, they borrow it from the surrounding code.
